reasoning: There is overwhelming evidence indicating that one or more UN member countries will formally recognize Palestine in June 2025. A UN-backed 'two-state solution' conference led by France and Saudi Arabia is scheduled for mid-June (around June 17-20). In the lead-up, France has been aggressively lobbying the UK, Canada, Belgium, and the Netherlands to partake in a joint recognition of Palestinian statehood at this conference. More concretely, on May 25, 2025, Malta's Prime Minister Robert Abela explicitly confirmed that Malta will officially recognize the State of Palestine on June 20. With multiple countries 'determined' to coordinate this in June and Malta specifically committing to a firmly scheduled date, the likelihood that at least one nation follows through within the calendar month is extremely high. The only remaining risk is a coordinated, last-minute delay to July due to intense diplomatic pressure or a postponement of the UN conference, but the sheer number of countries involved makes a total failure to act very unlikely.
